description: Test corpus for the logging-security skill. Fixtures are schema-driven code snippets; 'detect' marks code that leaks secrets/PII to logs or permits log injection (violates a NEVER), and 'ignore' marks code that redacts secrets, uses structured logging, and sanitizes user input (follows ALWAYS).

fixtures.reason: Logs a plaintext password (a field named 'password') to the log sink, leaking credentials. CWE-532 Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File; violates NEVER (log any field named password).

User-controlled 'q' is logged without stripping CR/LF/control chars, so an attacker can inject forged log lines. CWE-117 Improper Output Neutralization for Logs; violates ALWAYS (sanitize newlines from user-controlled strings).

Logs the full Authorization/Cookie headers and the full request body at INFO via console.log, leaking session tokens and PII. CWE-532; violates NEVER (log Authorization/Cookie headers, full request bodies, use console.log in production).
Structured log with stable fields, no credential logged; the password is excluded entirely. Follows ALWAYS (structured format, no secret fields). Secure per CWE-532.
User input has newlines/control chars stripped before logging, preventing forged log entries. Follows ALWAYS (sanitize newlines/control characters); safe per CWE-117.

Sensitive headers (Authorization, Cookie) are filtered out by a redactor before logging, and no request body is logged. Follows ALWAYS (run logs through a redactor, structured logging); safe per CWE-532.
